mission.title: Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
mission.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and operations of Kubernetes clusters in Azure. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Azure's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install Azure CLI
steps.description:
The Azure CLI is required to interact with Azure resources and create the AKS cluster.

```bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

```

> Install Azure CLI on Debian-based systems.

steps.title: Create an AKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new AKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

```

> Replace 'myResourceGroup' and 'myAKSCluster' with your desired names.
commands: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to connect to the newly created AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

```

> This merges the AKS cluster context into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command verifies that the nodes in the AKS cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up monitoring and autoscaling for your AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

az aks update --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --enable-cluster-autoscaler --min-count 1 --max-count 5

```

> Enables monitoring for the cluster.
> Enables cluster autoscaler.
commands: az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete AKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the AKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

az aks delete --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --yes --no-wait

```

> This will delete the cluster and orphan any associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade AKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your workloads, cordon the nodes, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o wide > backup_pods.txt

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --yes

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current pod states.
> Upgrade the AKS cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that the nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to get credentials for cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified resource group or cluster name may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the resource group and cluster name with 'az aks list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The requested node count exceeds the available resources in the specified region.

**Fix:**
Reduce the node count or choose a different region with available resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The nodes may be initializing or there may be a network issue.

**Fix:**
Check the AKS cluster status with 'az aks show --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to enable monitoring'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The monitoring add-on may not be supported in the current region.

**Fix:**
Check the supported regions for monitoring add-on and choose a different region if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the release notes for any breaking changes and update your manifests accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus
az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Azure CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
